faculty.summary: Faculty member in the Biophysics graduate field. Research interests not specified on program page.

faculty.name: Glenn E. Simmons
faculty.title: General Member
faculty.summary: Investigates lipid metabolism influence on tumor immunity and cellular plasticity in breast and lung cancers.

faculty.name: Erik Henning Thiede
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Integrates AI with physical simulation to understand protein conformational change, including Cryo-EM simulation and allosteric drug discovery.

faculty.name: Robert Edward Thorne
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies materials physics problems in structural genomics including protein phase behavior, purification, crystal disorders, radiation damage, cryopreservation, and instrumentation.

faculty.name: Michelle D. Wang
faculty.title: James Gilbert White Distinguished Professor, HHMI Investigator
faculty.summary: Research interests include molecular motors, mechanics of gene expression, RNA polymerase, and biopolymers using single-molecule approaches.

faculty.name: Zheng Jane Wang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on the physics of living organisms, particularly insect flight, fluid dynamics, and computational and applied mathematics.

faculty.name: Mingming Wu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies directed cell migration mediated by ligand-receptor binding in contexts of cancer biology, using microfluidics and optical microscopy.

faculty.name: Chunhui (Chris) Xu
faculty.title: IBM Professor of Engineering, Director of AEP
faculty.summary: Research interests include biomedical imaging, fiber optics, and optical instrumentation.

faculty.name: Haiyuan Yu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on quantitative biomedical systems biology, functional and comparative genomics/proteomics and biological networks across human populations.

faculty.name: Warren R. Zipfel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches optical microscopy, multiphoton imaging, fluorescence spectroscopy and bioanalytical methods applied to chromatin conformation and cancer biology.

funding.note: Fully funded 5 yrs (stipend, tuition, fees, health insurance)

faculty.name: Ehsan Samei
faculty.title: Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: Leading diagnostic medical physicist developing methods for CT dose optimization, image quality assessment, and patient-specific dosimetry; Chief of Clinical Imaging Physics at Duke.

faculty.name: Kathryn Nightingale
faculty.title: Professor of Biomedical Engineering
faculty.summary: Pioneer in acoustic radiation force impulse (ARFI) imaging and shear wave elasticity imaging for noninvasive tissue characterization.

faculty.name: Joseph Lo
faculty.title: Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: Medical physicist developing computer-aided detection and machine learning methods for breast imaging, mammography, and tomosynthesis.

faculty.name: Adam Wax
faculty.title: Professor of Biomedical Engineering
faculty.summary: Biomedical engineer developing novel optical spectroscopy and interferometry techniques for biomedical diagnostics and imaging.

faculty.name: Allen Song
faculty.title: Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: MRI physicist developing advanced functional and structural neuroimaging methods, including high-resolution fMRI and diffusion imaging.

faculty.name: Mark Oldham
faculty.title: Professor of Radiation Oncology
faculty.summary: Radiation oncology physicist pioneering 3D dosimetry techniques using optical CT and radiochromic materials for advanced radiotherapy QA.

faculty.name: Cristian Badea
faculty.title: Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: Medical physicist developing spectral and phase-contrast micro-CT techniques for preclinical imaging of cardiovascular and pulmonary disease in small animals.

faculty.name: Paul Segars
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: Developer of the XCAT family of computational human phantoms for medical imaging simulation and virtual clinical trials.

faculty.name: Bastiaan Driehuys
faculty.title: Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: Pioneer in hyperpolarized xenon-129 MRI for functional pulmonary imaging, enabling noninvasive assessment of lung ventilation and gas exchange.

faculty.name: Timothy Turkington
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: Nuclear medicine physicist developing PET and SPECT instrumentation, image reconstruction algorithms, and quantitative PET/MR methods.

faculty.name: Michael Zalutsky
faculty.title: Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: Radiochemist developing targeted radionuclide therapies, including radioimmunoconjugates for brain tumor treatment using astatine-211 and other alpha emitters.

faculty.name: Anuj Kapadia
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: Medical physicist developing novel X-ray diffraction and coherent scatter imaging for material-specific tissue characterization.

faculty.name: Scott Floyd
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Radiation Oncology
faculty.summary: Physician-scientist studying radiation biology mechanisms including DNA damage response and tumor microenvironment effects in radiation therapy.

faculty.name: Justus Adamson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Radiation Oncology
faculty.summary: Medical physicist developing patient-specific quality assurance methods for stereotactic radiosurgery and IMRT treatments.

faculty.name: Kyle Lafata
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Radiation Oncology
faculty.summary: Computational medical physicist applying radiomics, deep learning, and spatial statistics to predict treatment response and outcomes in radiation oncology.

faculty.name: Dan Ma
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: MRI physicist co-inventor of MR Fingerprinting, a quantitative imaging framework for simultaneous multi-parametric tissue property mapping.

faculty.name: Justin Solomon
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Radiology
faculty.summary: Medical physicist developing task-based image quality assessment methods and deep learning approaches for CT and other diagnostic modalities.

faculty.name: Gregory Palmer
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Radiation Oncology
faculty.summary: Radiation biologist and medical physicist studying tumor hypoxia and its impact on radiation response using optical imaging techniques.

faculty.name: Deshan Yang
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Radiation Oncology
faculty.summary: Medical physicist developing deformable image registration and adaptive radiotherapy methods for improving treatment accuracy.

faculty.name: Qiuwen Wu
faculty.title: Professor of Radiation Oncology
faculty.summary: Medical physicist developing intensity-modulated radiation therapy treatment planning optimization and motion management techniques.

funding.note: Fully funded 5 yrs (stipend, tuition, fees, health insurance)

faculty.name: Phillip S. Barbeau
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental nuclear and particle physicist working on neutrino physics and dark matter detection using low-background detectors.

faculty.name: Steffen A. Bass
faculty.title: Arts and Sciences Distinguished Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical nuclear physicist studying relativistic heavy-ion collisions, quark-gluon plasma, and QCD matter under extreme conditions.

faculty.name: Kenneth R. Brown
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; Michael J. Fitzpatrick Distinguished Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Quantum information scientist developing trapped-ion quantum computing hardware and quantum error correction protocols.

faculty.name: Shailesh Chandrasekharan
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist working on lattice gauge theories, quantum critical behavior in fermion systems, and numerical methods for strongly correlated systems.

faculty.name: Albert M. Chang
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist studying quantum transport in nanoscale electronic devices and low-dimensional systems.

faculty.name: Patrick Charbonneau
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical soft condensed matter physicist studying glass formation, colloidal systems, and protein crystallization using statistical mechanics.

faculty.name: Gleb Finkelstein
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist investigating quantum transport in nanoscale devices including carbon nanotubes and graphene.

faculty.name: Haiyan Gao
faculty.title: Robert C. Richardson Distinguished Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental nuclear physicist probing nucleon structure and the proton radius puzzle using electron scattering at Jefferson Lab.

faculty.name: Sara Haravifard
faculty.title: William M. Fairbank Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental condensed matter physicist synthesizing and characterizing frustrated quantum magnets using neutron and X-ray scattering.

faculty.name: Calvin R. Howell
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental nuclear physicist and director of Triangle Universities Nuclear Laboratory (TUNL), studying neutron-induced reactions and few-body nuclear systems.

faculty.name: Jungsang Kim
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; Schiciano Family Distinguished Professor of ECE
faculty.summary: Quantum physicist and co-founder of IonQ, developing scalable trapped-ion quantum computing and quantum networking technologies.

faculty.name: Ashutosh V. Kotwal
faculty.title: Fritz London Distinguished Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist leading precision measurements of the W boson mass and Higgs boson studies at the Tevatron (CDF) and LHC (ATLAS).

faculty.name: Christopher R. Monroe
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; Gilhuly Family Presidential Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneer in trapped-ion quantum computing and co-founder of IonQ, advancing quantum information processing with atomic ions.

faculty.name: Berndt Mueller
faculty.title: James B. Duke Distinguished Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Leading theoretical nuclear physicist studying quark-gluon plasma, relativistic heavy-ion collisions, and QCD at extreme energy densities.

faculty.name: M. Ronen Plesser
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical physicist working on string theory, mirror symmetry, and the interface between geometry and quantum field theory.

faculty.name: Kate Scholberg
faculty.title: Arts and Sciences Distinguished Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Experimental neutrino physicist studying supernova neutrinos and astrophysical neutrino sources with Super-Kamiokande and DUNE.

faculty.name: Daniel M. Scolnic
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Observational cosmologist using Type Ia supernovae to measure cosmic expansion, leading studies of the Hubble tension and dark energy.

faculty.name: Michael A. Troxel
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics (Associate Chair)
faculty.summary: Observational cosmologist using weak gravitational lensing to probe dark energy and the large-scale structure of the universe with DES and LSST.

faculty.name: Roxanne P. Springer
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Theoretical particle physicist applying effective field theory to QCD, neutrino interactions, and physics beyond the Standard Model.

faculty.name: Christopher Walter
faculty.title: Professor of Physics (Chair)
faculty.summary: Experimental particle physicist and department chair studying neutrino oscillations with Super-Kamiokande, T2K, and DUNE experiments.

funding.note: $42,000 stipend + full tuition + health insurance

faculty.name: Connie Roth
faculty.title: Professor and Department Chair
faculty.summary: Studies experimental soft condensed matter physics, specifically how polymer material properties are influenced by nanoconfinement, surfaces, interfaces, and external perturbations, with focus on glass transition and physical aging in non-equilibrium systems.

faculty.name: Keith Berland
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on molecular and cellular biophysics, developing novel methods in two-photon fluorescence imaging and spectroscopy applied to study biomolecular interactions and dynamics, including single-molecule detection techniques.

faculty.name: Gordon Berman
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Biology
faculty.summary: Studies animal behavior across entire behavioral repertoires, analyzing everything from insect biomechanics to neural control of behavior in fruit flies, aiming to uncover organizing principles of motion using computational and theoretical approaches.

faculty.name: Stefan Boettcher
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works in theoretical and computational physics focusing on strongly disordered systems with applications spanning evolutionary dynamics, amorphous materials, and combinatorial optimization.

faculty.name: Justin Burton
faculty.title: Professor, Winship Distinguished Research Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates experimental condensed matter and soft matter physics, exploring how fluid and granular flows self-organize, form patterns, and couple to other dynamical systems across scales from molecular to geophysical.

faculty.name: Tankut Can
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Applies theoretical physics and mathematical techniques to study how memory and computation work in both biological brains and artificial systems, at the intersection of AI, cognitive science, and neuroscience.

faculty.name: Katherine Davis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Studies experimental biophysics and biophysical chemistry, specifically metalloenzymes, developing spectroscopic and crystallographic methods to probe enzyme dynamics and understand how electronic and structural properties enable biological function.

faculty.name: Hayk Harutyunyan
faculty.title: Professor and Director of Graduate Studies
faculty.summary: Studies experimental condensed matter physics and nanoscience, investigating how light interacts with nanoscale matter and how shape, composition, and nonlinearities affect optical properties with applications in optoelectronics and imaging.

faculty.name: Minsu Kim
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on quantitative systems biology and single-cell microbiology, studying individual microbial cell behavior using quantitative approaches.

faculty.name: Jiang-Xiazi Lin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies emergent electronic phenomena in low-dimensional quantum materials, focusing on two-dimensional flat band systems where electron interactions create novel quantum phases through condensed matter experiment and quantum transport.

faculty.name: Ilya Nemenman
faculty.title: Samuel Candler Dobbs Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how biological systems process and respond to environmental information, focusing on computational primitives in living organisms, with expertise in information transduction and coarse-grained modeling in systems biology and neuroscience.

faculty.name: Jennifer Rieser
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of experimental soft condensed matter physics and organismal biophysics, exploring how physical properties of natural environments influence animal behaviors, morphological adaptations, and built structures.

faculty.name: Luiz Santos
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Theoretical condensed matter physicist studying topological phases of matter including Fractional Quantum Hall Effect, topological insulators and superconductors, and how symmetry constraints and lattice effects influence many-body entanglement.

faculty.name: Shashank Shekhar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies molecular and organismal biophysics, understanding how complex interactions among actin regulatory proteins and mechanical forces produce emergent cellular actin dynamics using biophysical experiments and mathematical modeling.

faculty.name: Daniel Sussman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works in theoretical and computational soft condensed matter, studying how disordered solids maintain and lose rigidity, active matter models of collective behavior (cells, flocks), and data-driven methods to test competing theories.

faculty.name: Sergei Urazhdin
faculty.title: Samuel Candler Dobbs Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Works in experimental condensed matter physics on spintronics, electronic and magnetic properties of surfaces and nanostructures, and nonlinear dynamics in nanomagnetic systems, exploring spin-polarized currents and spin-waves for information processing.

faculty.name: Nic Vega
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Biology
faculty.summary: Studies the ecology of host-associated microbial communities using C. elegans as a model system, including microbial community stability, phage dynamics in microbiomes, and evolution of antibiotic resistance in host-associated consortia.

faculty.name: Yao Wang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Chemistry
faculty.summary: Focuses on condensed matter theory and quantum information science, using computational methods to study quantum many-body systems and bridge experimental findings with theoretical frameworks for high-temperature superconductors and quantum computing.

faculty.name: Kurt Warncke
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on experimental biophysics using pulsed-EPR and optical studies of metallocenter- and radical-mediated enzyme and chemical catalyses, particularly employing time-resolved approaches.

faculty.name: Eric Weeks
faculty.title: Samuel Candler Dobbs Professor
faculty.summary: Studies experimental soft condensed matter physics using microscopy to investigate colloidal glasses, nonlinear dynamics, complex fluids, and granular media, understanding how materials like foams, sand piles, and glasses behave out of equilibrium.

faculty.name: Daniel Weissman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works in theoretical physics and evolution, building models to predict biological population evolution, including inferring dynamics from genomic data and understanding complex adaptations requiring multiple mutations, with applications like predicting antibiotic resistance.

faculty.name: Wladimir Benalcazar
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works in condensed matter theory on how topology, symmetries, geometry, and interactions give rise to topological phases in insulators, superconductors, and metamaterials including photonic and acoustic crystals.

funding.note: Assistantships/fellowships available; see department graduate fellowships page

requirements.admission_model: School graduate committee recommends admission to Office of Graduate Education; recurring Dec 15 deadline (year not posted)

funding.note: Unknown/Verify
Fully funded (SEAS PhD guarantee: tuition + stipend)

faculty.name: Federico Capasso
faculty.title: Robert L. Wallace Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Co-inventor of the quantum cascade laser, which revolutionized mid-infrared and terahertz photonics. More recently pioneered metasurface-based flat optics. Recipient of dozens of major awards including the Balzan Prize, Rumford Prize, and the IEEE Edison Medal. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: David A. Weitz
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Co-director of the BASF Advanced Research Initiative at Harvard. Pioneered droplet-based microfluidics, microrheology, and passive particle tracking. His technologies are used globally for single-cell genomics, drug screening, and materials science. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Eric Mazur
faculty.title: Balkanski Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Pioneer of femtosecond laser materials processing, discovering 'black silicon' and developing applications in photodetectors. Also world-renowned for inventing Peer Instruction, a widely adopted interactive teaching methodology. Member of the National Academy of Education.

faculty.name: Conor J. Walsh
faculty.title: Paul A. Maeder Professor of Engineering and Applied Sciences
faculty.summary: Global leader in soft wearable robotics for human augmentation and rehabilitation. His soft exosuits use textiles and soft actuation to assist mobility-impaired individuals. Work has direct clinical impact for stroke and ALS patients.

faculty.name: Lene Vestergaard Hau
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Famous for experimentally stopping light in a Bose-Einstein condensate — a landmark demonstration of quantum optical control. Research has profound implications for quantum information storage and quantum networks. MacArthur Fellow.

faculty.name: Philip Kim
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Leading experimentalist in graphene and 2D materials. His measurements of graphene's electronic properties were foundational to the field. Now working on correlated and topological phases in moiré heterostructures. Member of the National Academy of Sciences. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Evelyn Hu
faculty.title: Tarr-Coyne Professor of Applied Physics and of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Pioneer in nanofabrication and quantum devices. Her group develops semiconductor-based quantum photonic devices, including cavity-coupled defect spin qubits in SiC. Major contributions to III-V semiconductor device physics. MacArthur Fellow (1993). Member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Vinothan N. Manoharan
faculty.title: Wagner Family Professor of Chemical Engineering and of Physics
faculty.summary: Area Chair for Applied Physics at SEAS. Studies how colloidal particles self-assemble into complex structures with novel optical properties. His work bridges fundamental soft matter physics with photonic materials and biological assembly.

faculty.name: Zhigang Suo
faculty.title: Allen E. and Marilyn M. Puckett Professor of Mechanics and Materials
faculty.summary: World-leading mechanician studying the deformation, fracture, and adhesion of soft materials. Pioneered highly stretchable and tough hydrogels, ionotronics, and dielectric elastomers. His work enables soft, biocompatible devices and artificial muscles. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Donhee Ham
faculty.title: John A. and Elizabeth S. Armstrong Professor of Engineering and Applied Sciences
faculty.summary: Leading researcher bridging integrated circuits and quantum engineering. His group develops cryogenic CMOS control electronics for quantum processors and advanced biosensing platforms. Major innovations in brain-on-chip technologies.

faculty.name: Frans A. Spaepen
faculty.title: John C. and Helen F. Franklin Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Senior figure in materials physics known for foundational work on metallic glasses, colloidal crystals as model systems for studying crystallization, and the thermodynamics of interfaces. Director of the Harvard Materials Research Science and Engineering Center.

faculty.name: Joanna Aizenberg
faculty.title: Amy Smith Berylson Professor of Materials Science; Professor of Chemistry and Chemical Biology
faculty.summary: Pioneer of bio-inspired materials science. Invented SLIPS (Slippery Liquid-Infused Porous Surfaces), a revolutionary omniphobic coating inspired by pitcher plants. Research spans self-healing materials, adaptive optics, and bio-inspired surface engineering. Member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Jennifer Lewis
faculty.title: Hansjorg Wyss Professor of Biologically Inspired Engineering
faculty.summary: World leader in 3D printing of functional, structural, and biological materials. Pioneered multimaterial 3D printing for soft robotics, printed electronics, vascularized tissues, and structural materials. Her innovations are commercialized through multiple startups. Member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Kit Parker
faculty.title: Tarr Family Professor of Bioengineering and Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Army veteran and Harvard professor who builds human heart tissue on chips to study cardiac disease and drug responses. His group bridges fundamental biophysics with tissue engineering, creating models of neuromuscular and cardiac diseases. Multiple startup companies spun out.

faculty.name: Efthimios Kaxiras
faculty.title: John Hasbrouck Van Vleck Professor of Pure and Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Computational physicist developing and applying first-principles electronic structure methods to a broad range of materials problems. Work spans 2D materials, energy conversion, catalysts, and quantum materials. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Julia A. Mundy
faculty.title: John L. Loeb Associate Professor of the Natural Sciences and of Engineering and Applied Sciences
faculty.summary: Rising-star experimentalist designing and synthesizing atomically perfect complex oxide heterostructures using molecular beam epitaxy. Her group creates and probes emergent quantum phases at oxide interfaces. Packard Fellow and Sloan Fellow.

faculty.name: David J. Mooney
faculty.title: Robert P. Pinkas Family Professor of Bioengineering
faculty.summary: Leader in biomaterials for immunoengineering and tissue regeneration. His group developed implantable biomaterial scaffolds that program immune cells in situ for cancer immunotherapy — a transformational approach now in clinical trials. Member of the National Academy of Engineering.

faculty.name: Katia Bertoldi
faculty.title: William and Ami Kuan Danoff Professor of Applied Mechanics
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in mechanical metamaterials — architected materials with extraordinary mechanical properties derived from their structure rather than composition. Her work on buckling-induced auxetic materials, programmable metamaterials, and soft robotic structures is highly influential.

faculty.name: Boris Kozinsky
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Materials Science and Mechanical Engineering; Professor of Chemistry and Chemical Biology
faculty.summary: Leader in combining machine learning with first-principles computation for accelerated materials discovery. Research focuses on battery materials, catalysts, and quantum materials. Formerly at Bosch Research, bridging academia and industrial applications.

faculty.name: Michael J. Aziz
faculty.title: Gene and Tracy Sykes Professor of Materials and Energy Technologies
faculty.summary: Pioneer in aqueous organic redox flow batteries for grid-scale energy storage. His group develops long-lifetime organic molecules for cost-effective large-scale batteries. Also known for foundational work on ion-beam processing and laser annealing of semiconductors.

faculty.name: Daniel Needleman
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Applied Physics and Professor of Molecular and Cellular Biology
faculty.summary: Biophysicist studying the physical principles of self-organization in living cells, particularly the mitotic spindle that segregates chromosomes during cell division. His interdisciplinary work spans physics, biology, and quantitative imaging.

faculty.name: Joost J. Vlassak
faculty.title: Abbott and James Lawrence Professor of Materials Engineering
faculty.summary: Expert in mechanical behavior of thin films and small-scale materials. Research on reliability of microelectronic materials, MEMS devices, and metallization. His combinatorial nanocalorimetry technique enables rapid materials characterization.

faculty.name: Philippe Cluzel
faculty.title: Gordon McKay Professor of Applied Physics and Professor of Molecular and Cellular Biology
faculty.summary: Quantitative biologist studying how cells process information and respond to environmental signals. His group combines microfluidics, time-lapse microscopy, and modeling to understand gene regulation dynamics at the single-cell level.

faculty.name: Zachary Schiffer
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Early-career researcher developing new electrochemical processes for sustainable chemical manufacturing. His research uses fundamental electrochemistry and materials science to enable cleaner production of chemicals and fuels.

faculty.name: Maxim Prigozhin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Molecular and Cellular Biology and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Develops new tools combining cryo-EM structural biology with protein engineering to visualize and control cellular signaling. His work enables optical control of specific proteins with potential therapeutic applications.

funding.note: Fully funded (GSAS PhD guarantee)
Fully funded (GSAS PhD guarantee)

faculty.name: Adam E. Cohen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops new physical tools to study molecules and cells, including the first fluorescent voltage-indicating proteins that enable all-optical mapping of neural activity. Holds joint appointments in Chemistry & Chemical Biology and Physics.

faculty.name: Eric J. Heller
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneers time-dependent wavepacket methods for molecular spectroscopy and reaction dynamics. Known for discovering quantum scarring of wavefunctions by unstable periodic orbits, and extending wave mechanics to electron flow in 2D materials and graphene quantum dots. Earned his PhD in Chemical Physics from Harvard.

faculty.name: Hongkun Park
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the interface of quantum materials, nanophotonics, and bio-nano interfaces. Pioneered single-molecule transistors and developed diamond NV-center quantum sensors for room-temperature NMR detection of single proteins. Currently on leave as Corporate President of Samsung Electronics.

faculty.name: Kang-Kuen Ni
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Creates ultracold molecules at temperatures near absolute zero to study quantum-controlled chemical reactions. Recently demonstrated a two-qubit entangling quantum gate using individually controlled molecules, advancing molecular quantum information science.

faculty.name: Joonho Lee
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theoretical and computational methods for electron-electron and electron-vibration correlation problems. Applies quantum chemistry and quantum computing methods to energy-relevant materials including perovskites, moire materials, and electrocatalysis. Also a visiting researcher at Google Quantum AI.

faculty.name: Eugene I. Shakhnovich
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses statistical mechanics, Monte Carlo simulations, and machine learning to understand protein folding principles, the relationship between amino acid sequence and structure, and how protein stability mutations affect organismal fitness.

faculty.name: Boris Kozinsky
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Materials Intelligence Research group developing ML-accelerated atomistic and electronic structure methods for functional materials. Creator of the NequIP and Allegro neural network interatomic potentials, applied to catalysts, batteries, and 2D materials. Joint appointment with SEAS.

faculty.name: Jarad A. Mason
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Designs new classes of porous materials including liquids with intrinsic microporosity, microporous nanocrystals for gas transport in aqueous environments, and barocaloric materials for sustainable solid-state heating and cooling. Applies synthetic chemistry and nanoscience to challenges in energy, sustainability, and medicine.

faculty.name: Frank N. Keutsch
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies photochemical oxidation of volatile organic compounds that produce tropospheric ozone and secondary organic aerosol, combining field observations aboard NASA aircraft, laboratory kinetics studies, and laser-induced fluorescence instrument development. Also leads the SCoPEx solar geoengineering research project.

faculty.name: Daniel G. Nocera
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneered the artificial leaf — a silicon solar cell coated with self-healing catalysts that splits water into hydrogen and oxygen at high efficiency. Extended this to the bionic leaf, integrating engineered bacteria to produce liquid fuels and renewable ammonia fertilizer from sunlight, air, and water.

faculty.name: James G. Anderson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneered measurements of chlorine radical chain reactions that directly linked CFCs to stratospheric ozone depletion, informing the Montreal Protocol. Develops advanced laser, lidar, and high-altitude aircraft instrumentation to study climate-radiation-dynamics coupling in the atmosphere.

faculty.name: Joanna Aizenberg
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneers biomimetic materials chemistry, studying biological architectures such as biominerals and using those principles to design bio-inspired synthetic materials. Inventor of SLIPS (Slippery Liquid-Infused Porous Surfaces), omniphobic self-healing coatings inspired by pitcher plants. Holds joint appointments with SEAS and the Wyss Institute.

faculty.name: George M. Whitesides
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Developed foundational technologies including self-assembled monolayers on gold, soft lithography for nanofabrication, and paper-based microfluidic diagnostics. Currently investigates the origin of life through prebiotic chemistry, with over 1,200 publications and the highest Hirsch index of any living chemist.

faculty.name: Xiaowei Zhuang
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Invented STORM super-resolution microscopy that overcomes the diffraction limit, and MERFISH for genome-scale single-cell imaging of thousands of RNA species simultaneously. Created a comprehensive cell atlas of the entire adult mouse brain. Joint appointment in Chemistry & Chemical Biology and Physics.

faculty.name: Efthimios Kaxiras
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Holds the John Hasbrouck Van Vleck Professorship in Physics with an affiliate appointment in Chemistry and Chemical Biology. Works on electronic properties of solids, graphene and 2D materials, and machine learning for materials modeling. Founding Director of the Institute for Applied Computational Science.

faculty.name: Roy G. Gordon
faculty.title: Professor Emeritus
faculty.summary: Studied chemical physics at Harvard under Nobel laureate J.H. Van Vleck. Research spans quantum mechanics, spectroscopy, chemical vapor deposition of thin films for solar cells and energy-efficient windows, and flow batteries for grid-scale energy storage. Elected to National Academy of Sciences in 1975.

faculty.name: Dudley R. Herschbach
faculty.title: Professor Emeritus
faculty.summary: Nobel Laureate (1986) who pioneered crossed molecular beam experiments that revealed detailed molecular-level mechanisms of elementary chemical reactions in single collisions. His work uncovered both rebound and stripping reaction dynamics, fundamentally transforming the field of chemical reaction dynamics.

faculty.name: Cynthia M. Friend
faculty.title: Professor Emerita
faculty.summary: First female full professor of chemistry at Harvard (1989). Research focused on heterogeneous catalysis on coinage metals and their alloys, developing selective catalytic syntheses. Directed the DOE Energy Frontier Research Center IMASC on mesoscale architectures for sustainable catalysis. Now President of the Kavli Foundation.

funding.note: Fully funded (GSAS PhD guarantee)

faculty.name: Cumrun Vafa
faculty.title: Hollis Professor of Mathematicks and Natural Philosophy; Department Chair
faculty.summary: One of the most influential string theorists in the world. Originator of F-theory and the swampland program. Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics (2017) and Dirac Medal (2008). Member of the National Academy of Sciences.

faculty.name: Andrew Strominger
faculty.title: Gwill E. York Professor of Physics; Director, Center for the Fundamental Laws of Nature
faculty.summary: Towering figure in quantum gravity. Made foundational contributions to black hole physics, including the Strominger-Vafa entropy counting and recent work on black hole soft hair and asymptotic symmetries. Breakthrough Prize (2017).

faculty.name: Mikhail Lukin
faculty.title: Joshua and Beth Friedman University Professor
faculty.sub_field: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics / Quantum Information
faculty.summary: Leading experimentalist in quantum optics and quantum information science. Co-director of the Harvard Quantum Initiative. Pioneered quantum simulation with Rydberg atom arrays and quantum networking with diamond color centers. Member of the National Academy of Sciences. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Subir Sachdev
faculty.title: Herchel Smith Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: World-leading condensed matter theorist. Author of the definitive textbook on quantum phase transitions. Developed the SYK model for strange metals and made foundational contributions to understanding high-temperature superconductivity. Dirac Medal (2018). Member of the National Academy of Sciences.

faculty.name: Lisa Randall
faculty.title: Frank B. Baird, Jr. Professor of Science
faculty.sub_field: High Energy Theory / Particle Physics / Cosmology
faculty.summary: One of the most cited particle physicists. Co-originator of the Randall-Sundrum warped extra dimensions model. Author of influential popular science books. Research spans particle physics, cosmology, and dark matter. Member of the National Academy of Sciences.

faculty.name: Philip Kim
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Pioneer in graphene and 2D materials research. Led early experiments measuring graphene's remarkable electronic properties. Now working on moiré quantum matter. Highly Cited Researcher and member of the National Academy of Sciences.

faculty.name: Ashvin Vishwanath
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Prominent condensed matter theorist who predicted Weyl semimetals and contributed foundational ideas to topological insulators and the theory of moiré heterostructures. Highly Cited Researcher. Recipient of the Rahman Prize (2025).

faculty.name: Howard Georgi
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Physics
faculty.sub_field: High Energy Theory / Quantum Field Theory
faculty.summary: Pioneer of Grand Unified Theories (GUTs) and co-developer of the Georgi-Glashow SU(5) model. Major contributions to effective field theory and quantum field theory. Longtime Director of Undergraduate Studies and legendary pedagogical influence.

faculty.name: John Doyle
faculty.title: Henry B. Silsbee Professor of Physics
faculty.sub_field: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics / Precision Measurement
faculty.summary: Leading experimentalist in cold molecule physics and precision tests of fundamental symmetries. Co-director of the ACME collaboration measuring the electron electric dipole moment. 2024 Norman F. Ramsey Prize recipient. Member of the National Academy of Sciences.

faculty.name: Markus Greiner
faculty.title: George Vasmer Leverett Professor of Physics
faculty.sub_field: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics / Quantum Simulation
faculty.summary: Pioneer of the quantum gas microscope technique, which enables single-atom, single-site resolved imaging of ultracold atoms in optical lattices. This has become one of the most powerful platforms for experimental quantum simulation. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: David A. Weitz
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: World leader in soft condensed matter physics. Pioneered droplet-based microfluidics and its applications in biology and materials science. His group develops widely used tools for high-throughput single-cell analysis. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Daniel Jafferis
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Rising star in theoretical physics. Known for groundbreaking work on the connection between wormholes and quantum teleportation, and for the Gao-Jafferis-Wall traversable wormhole protocol. Frontiers of Science Award recipient.

faculty.name: Matthew Schwartz
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Leading expert in precision QCD calculations for the LHC. Author of the widely adopted graduate textbook 'Quantum Field Theory and the Standard Model.' Research on jet physics, precision collider phenomenology, and effective field theories.

faculty.name: Amir Yacoby
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Leading experimentalist in quantum condensed matter systems. Research on spin qubits in quantum dots, topological insulators, and quantum Hall edge states. Co-developed scanning NV magnetometry techniques for probing magnetic materials.

faculty.name: Norman Yao
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Brilliant theorist working at the intersection of AMO physics, condensed matter, and quantum information. Pioneered Floquet time crystals and made key contributions to many-body localization and quantum sensing protocols. PECASE award (2025). Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Melissa Franklin
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: First woman tenured in Harvard Physics (1992). Key contributor to the CDF experiment that provided early evidence for the top quark. Currently co-director of the Harvard LPPC and leading ATLAS experiment research. Breakthrough Prize recipient (ATLAS collaboration).

faculty.name: Matthew Reece
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Prominent theorist working on particle physics beyond the Standard Model. Key contributor to axion physics, dark matter model building, and precision tests of fundamental physics. Sloan Fellow.

faculty.name: Kang-Kuen Ni
faculty.title: Professor of Chemistry and Chemical Biology; Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Pioneer in assembling individual ultracold molecules from atoms using optical tweezers, enabling full quantum control of molecular quantum states. Opens new frontiers in quantum chemistry and dipolar quantum simulation.

faculty.name: Jenny Hoffman
faculty.title: Clowes Professor of Science
faculty.summary: Expert in scanning tunneling microscopy of quantum materials, including high-temperature superconductors and topological materials. Co-author of the highly influential textbook 'The Physics and Chemistry of Materials.' Also known for completing a cross-country run across the US.

faculty.name: John Huth
faculty.title: Donner Professor of Science
faculty.summary: Longtime ATLAS collaborator studying electroweak physics and Higgs boson production at the LHC. Recipient of the U.S. ATLAS Lifetime Achievement Award. Also known for teaching a popular course on primitive navigation.

faculty.name: Lene Vestergaard Hau
faculty.title: Mallinckrodt Professor of Physics and of Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Famous for experimentally slowing light to 17 meters per second and stopping it entirely in a Bose-Einstein condensate. These dramatic demonstrations opened new possibilities for quantum information storage and manipulation.

faculty.name: Xiaowei Zhuang
faculty.title: David B. Arnold Jr. Professor of Science; Professor of Chemistry and Chemical Biology and of Physics
faculty.summary: Inventor of STORM (stochastic optical reconstruction microscopy), a revolutionary super-resolution imaging technique that broke the diffraction limit. Breakthrough Prize in Life Sciences (2019). Member of the National Academy of Sciences. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Efthimios Kaxiras
faculty.title: John Hasbrouck Van Vleck Professor of Pure and Applied Physics
faculty.summary: Leading computational physicist developing and applying first-principles methods to study condensed matter systems. Wide-ranging research spanning 2D materials, energy materials, and complex oxides. Highly Cited Researcher.

faculty.name: Carlos Argüelles-Delgado
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Rising star in neutrino physics and multi-messenger astronomy. Group works on IceCube neutrino observatory physics, quantum decoherence searches in neutrino oscillations, and detecting supernova neutrinos with the LHC detectors.

faculty.name: Jordan Cotler
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Sloan Fellow (2024) working at the exciting intersection of quantum information theory, quantum gravity, and high-energy physics. Made key contributions connecting the SYK model, black holes, and quantum chaos.

programs.program_name: Biology (Cellular, Molecular, Developmental Biology and Biophysics), PhD

funding.note: Fully funded
Fully funded

faculty.name: Doug Barrick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Statistical Thermodynamics, Protein Folding and Design
faculty.summary: Studies protein folding thermodynamics and design using repeat proteins as model systems, and investigates conformational transitions in the Notch signaling pathway that control cell fate determination. Authored the textbook Biothermodynamics: from Theory to Application.

faculty.name: Gregory Bowman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the structure and mechanisms of ATP-dependent chromatin remodelers such as Chd1 that reposition nucleosomes along DNA, using cryo-EM and X-ray crystallography to understand the twist-defect mechanism of nucleosome sliding.

faculty.name: Brian Camley
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses theory, computation, and simulation to study the physics of cell biology — how cells crawl, sense chemical gradients and electric fields, and migrate collectively through complex environments. Joint appointment with Physics and Astronomy.

faculty.name: Karen Fleming
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the physical principles of membrane protein folding and biogenesis in lipid bilayers, including chaperone functions and the BAM complex in Gram-negative bacteria. Elected AAAS Fellow in 2024 and received the Biophysical Society Avanti Award in Lipids.

faculty.name: Bertrand Garcia-Moreno
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies electrostatic effects in proteins using experimental and computational methods, focusing on buried ionizable groups in hydrophobic environments and engineering pH-sensitive switches for therapeutic applications. Serves as Executive Vice Dean of the Krieger School.

faculty.name: Thomas Graham
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops proximity-assisted photoactivation (PAPA) and single-molecule tracking methods to visualize individual endogenous molecular complexes regulating transcription in live cells. Joined the department in July 2025.

faculty.name: Yuan He
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Uses cryo-electron microscopy and biochemical approaches to study how cells read, write, and repair their genomes, with focus on transcription initiation complexes and DNA double-strand break repair. Joined Johns Hopkins in January 2025 as a Bloomberg Distinguished Professor with appointments in Biophysics and Biology.

faculty.name: William Huang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates biochemical reactions at the cell membrane using reconstitution and single-molecule approaches, focusing on kinetic proofreading mechanisms in Ras GTPase activation and membrane localization effects on signaling.

faculty.name: Margaret Johnson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops computational and theoretical models of protein self-assembly on membranes, focusing on clathrin-mediated endocytosis and HIV-1 Gag lattice assembly. Creator of the NERDSS reaction-diffusion simulation package.

faculty.name: Albert Lau
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Uses molecular dynamics simulations and free energy calculations on supercomputers to study atomic mechanisms of ionotropic glutamate receptors that mediate excitatory synaptic transmission, informing drug discovery for neurological disorders.

faculty.name: Juliette Lecomte
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses NMR spectroscopy and other biophysical methods to investigate the structure, dynamics, and function of heme proteins, particularly truncated hemoglobins from bacteria, cyanobacteria, algae, and deep-sea extremophiles. Elected AAAS Fellow in 2023.

faculty.name: Evgenia Nikolova
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structure and dynamics of nucleic acids and their protein complexes, focusing on how pioneer transcription factors such as Sox2 and Oct4 engage nucleosomes to remodel chromatin and activate gene expression. Joined the department in 2017.

faculty.name: Sarah Woodson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies how RNA molecules fold into three-dimensional structures and how RNA-protein complexes such as the ribosome assemble, using single-molecule fluorescence, X-ray footprinting, and SAXS. Also investigates small non-coding RNAs and the Hfq chaperone in bacteria.

faculty.name: Yaojun Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Develops theoretical and computational models of biomolecular phase separation using statistical mechanics and polymer theory, studying how condensates form, the interface resistance that controls material exchange, and how phase separation organizes intracellular space. Named a 2025 Sloan Research Fellow.

funding.note: Fully funded

faculty.name: Xun Jia
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Chief of Medical Physics Division. Develops GPU-accelerated Monte Carlo radiation transport simulations, AI-driven CBCT reconstruction, deep learning for treatment planning, and preclinical FLASH radiotherapy technologies. AIMBE Fellow, FAAPM, over 300 publications and 7,500+ citations.

faculty.name: Kai Ding
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Founding Co-Director of the Medical Physics PhD Program. Directs the Smart Biomaterial & Image-Guided Radiotherapy Lab, developing X-ray-based intra-operative image-guided platforms (XIOSIS) for smart material delivery and AI-based tumor contouring.

faculty.name: Todd McNutt
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Clinical Informatics and Director of the Medical Physics Residency. Creator of Oncospace (now Plan AI), an FDA-approved AI-powered informatics system that uses data from over 5,000 patients to optimize radiation treatment plans. Acquired by Sun Nuclear/Mirion Medical in 2025.

faculty.name: Heng Li
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Proton Physics and Vice Chief of Physics/Clinical-NCR at the Johns Hopkins National Proton Center. Leads commissioning of ultra-high dose rate (UHDR) proton beamlines for FLASH research, real-time gated proton therapy, and spatially fractionated proton therapy clinical trials. FAAPM, 142+ publications.

faculty.name: Robert Hobbs
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Radiation Safety. Develops microscale dosimetry models for alpha-particle emitting radionuclides, including nephron-based kidney and bone marrow toxicity models. Chair of AAPM committee on Radionuclide Therapy and elected MIRD Committee member.

faculty.name: Junghoon Lee
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Leads the Medical Imaging and Computing Analysis (MICA) lab. Develops real-time intraoperative dosimetry systems (iRUF) for prostate brachytherapy, automatic seed reconstruction algorithms, and multimodality image registration for brachytherapy treatment planning. 145+ publications.

faculty.name: Weihua Mao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Specializes in MRI-guided adaptive radiotherapy on MR-linac systems, markerless lung tumor tracking using EPID imaging, CBCT reconstruction for soft-tissue visualization, and real-time motion management for pancreas and lung cancers. 119 publications, 2,000+ citations.

faculty.name: Wil Ngwa
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.sub_field: Biomaterial Drones & Global Health Radiation Oncology
faculty.summary: PI of the Biomaterial Drones and Radio-immunotherapy Lab. Develops implantable biomaterial drones for smart delivery of immunoadjuvants combined with radiotherapy to trigger in-situ vaccination against metastatic disease. Director of Global Health Catalyst; Chair, Lancet Oncology Commission for Sub-Saharan Africa.

faculty.name: Sarah Han-Oh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Tumor Motion Management & Pancreatic Cancer Radiotherapy
faculty.summary: Vice Chief of Medical Physics Division (Baltimore). Develops novel tumor tracking technologies including microwave radar fiducial localization and ultrasound-based intra-fraction motion tracking for pancreatic cancer SBRT. Active APEx surveyor for ASTRO accreditation.

faculty.name: Michael Roumeliotis
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Director of Brachytherapy Physics. Advances AI-based auto-segmentation (DCT-UNet) for MRI-guided cervical cancer brachytherapy, robotic bronchoscopy-guided lung brachytherapy, and ultrasound-compatible gynecologic brachytherapy applicators.

faculty.name: Mohammad Rezaee
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Invented an orthovoltage x-ray FLASH irradiator for preclinical research and established the FLASH radiation research program at Johns Hopkins. Investigates DNA damage mechanisms from high-LET particles, UHDR proton FLASH dosimetry, and platinum-based radiosensitizers.

faculty.name: William T. Hrinivich
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Proton Therapy & Deep Reinforcement Learning Planning
faculty.summary: Medical physicist at the Johns Hopkins Proton Therapy Center. Develops GPU-based ultra-fast proton pencil beam dose calculation algorithms, deep reinforcement learning for automated VMAT/IMPT planning, and custom hardware solutions (table-mounted range shifter boards) for pediatric craniospinal irradiation.

faculty.name: Rachel B. Ger
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Focuses on quantitative imaging biomarkers (radiomics) for cancer prognosis, proton dose evaluation and range uncertainty analysis, deformable image registration accuracy, and stem cell-sparing radiotherapy to reduce xerostomia in head and neck cancer patients.

faculty.name: Khadija Sheikh
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Program Director of the Medical Physics Residency. Specializes in breath-hold techniques for breast proton therapy, daily CBCT-based adaptive planning workflows for proton treatment, and pediatric proton therapy planning optimization.

faculty.name: An Qin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.sub_field: Proton Arc Therapy & Deformable Image Registration
faculty.summary: Develops spot-scanning proton arc therapy (SPArc) techniques for head and neck, lung, and prostate cancers. Works on deformable image registration for tumor/organ shrinkage monitoring, CBCT-based treatment dose monitoring, and U-net-generated synthetic CT for MRI-only treatment planning.

programs.program_name: Molecular Biophysics, PhD (Program in Molecular Biophysics)

funding.note: Fully funded

faculty.name: Cynthia Wolberger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies the structural and mechanistic basis for transcriptional regulation and ubiquitin signaling, using X-ray crystallography and cryo-EM to understand how histone modifications control gene expression and DNA repair.

faculty.name: James M. Berger
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how multi-subunit assemblies use ATP to control DNA replication and chromosome superstructure, with a focus on replication initiation, replisome assembly, and topoisomerases as anti-cancer drug targets.

faculty.name: Jie Xiao
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Develops advanced single-molecule and superresolution imaging approaches to study molecular mechanisms of bacterial cell division, transcription regulation, and chromosome organization in living cells.

faculty.name: Doug Barrick
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies tandem repeat proteins as model systems to quantify cooperativity in protein folding, using nearest-neighbor Ising models and consensus repeat design to understand folding energy landscapes and pathway selection.

faculty.name: Karen Fleming
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the physical principles governing membrane protein folding, stability, and assembly in bacterial outer membranes, studying how lipid bilayers and periplasmic chaperones catalyze beta-barrel protein biogenesis.

faculty.name: Juliette Lecomte
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Uses NMR spectroscopy and other biophysical methods to study heme protein structure, stability, and dynamics, including truncated hemoglobins from microorganisms and their roles in reactive nitrogen species management.

faculty.name: Sarah Woodson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Studies RNA folding, RNA-protein interactions, and ribosome assembly using single-molecule fluorescence methods, with a focus on how the Hfq chaperone facilitates small RNA-mRNA pairing and how ribosomal proteins assemble co-transcriptionally.

faculty.name: Gregory Bowman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates the molecular mechanisms of ATP-dependent chromatin remodelers such as Chd1 that reposition nucleosomes along DNA, using cryo-EM and single-molecule approaches to understand how these motors control DNA accessibility.

faculty.name: Yuan He
faculty.title: Bloomberg Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Uses cryo-EM and AI-based tools to elucidate the molecular mechanisms of large multi-subunit complexes involved in eukaryotic gene transcription and DNA damage repair, including the transcription pre-initiation complex and NHEJ machinery.

faculty.name: Edward Twomey
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops time-resolved cryo-EM methods to capture dynamic states of glutamate receptors, revealing how neurotransmitter binding gates ion channel opening at physiological temperatures, with implications for neurological disorders.

faculty.name: Bin Wu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops single-molecule microscopy methods to visualize RNA birth, trafficking, translation, and decay in live cells and neurons, including the SINAPS technique for real-time imaging of translation on single mRNAs.

faculty.name: Albert Lau
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Combines computational and experimental approaches to study the atomic details of ionotropic glutamate receptors, investigating conformational dynamics that underlie synaptic transmission and identifying novel drug-binding sites.

faculty.name: Jungsan Sohn
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Investigates how innate immune stress sensors assemble, detect danger signals, and initiate responses, focusing on inflammasome formation, self vs. non-self DNA discrimination, and phase separation in DNA sensing pathways.

faculty.name: Dominique Frueh
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Develops novel NMR methods to study protein dynamics in large enzymatic systems, focusing on the molecular mechanisms of nonribosomal peptide synthetases that produce antibiotics and other bioactive compounds.

faculty.name: Chang Liu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Uses cryo-EM and cryo-ET to study coronavirus-host interactions, including the structural basis of SARS-CoV-2 proofreading, mechanisms of antiviral drug resistance, and modulation of host transcription by viral proteins.

funding.note: All admitted PhD students fully supported (TA/RA/fellowship, tuition + health benefits)

faculty.name: Bitan Roy
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: NSF CAREER awardee (2023). Studies topological phases of matter, superconductivity, magnetism, and disordered systems using field-theoretic and numerical approaches.

faculty.name: Ivan Biaggio
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; Dept. Chair, Joseph A. Waldschmitt Chair in Physics
faculty.summary: Department Chair and Optica Fellow. Studies organic semiconductors, exciton physics, entangled spin states, and integrated photonics.

faculty.name: Volkmar Dierolf
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; AGF Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Distinguished University Professor. Studies rare-earth atoms in semiconductors, light-emitting diodes, and quantum information storage.

faculty.name: Elliott Rosenberg
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies high-temperature superconductors and excitonic insulators using single crystal growth and strain-based measurement techniques.

faculty.name: Jeffrey Rickman
faculty.title: Professor of Physics and Materials Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Joint appointment in Physics and Materials Science. Studies materials physics with computational approaches to microstructure and properties.

faculty.name: Michael Stavola
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; Sherman Fairchild Chair in Physics
faculty.summary: Sherman Fairchild Chair. Studies defects and impurities in semiconductors using vibrational spectroscopy.

faculty.name: Ariel Sommer
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Uses ultracold lithium-6 Fermi gases to simulate strongly correlated materials, electron transport, and normal-superconductor junctions.

faculty.name: Coraline Fujiwara
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies optical lattices, topological phenomena, Floquet engineering, and quantum thermodynamics with ultracold atoms.

faculty.name: Sera Cremonini
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Works on string theory, black holes, and connections between quantum field theory and gravity, with techniques applicable to condensed matter physics.

faculty.name: Timm Wrase
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies string theory, early universe evolution, inflation, and dark energy.

faculty.name: Rosi Reed
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies quark-gluon plasma and heavy ion physics using experiments at Brookhaven National Lab's RHIC.

faculty.name: Anders Knospe
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies quark-gluon plasma and heavy ion collisions, with work on detector development at Brookhaven.

faculty.name: M. Virginia McSwain
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Observational astrophysicist studying massive binary stars and stellar evolution.

faculty.name: Daniel Ou-Yang
faculty.title: Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies colloids, macromolecule diffusion, and soft matter physics using optical tweezing techniques.

faculty.name: Aurelia Honerkamp-Smith
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Studies lipid membranes, nanoscale flow, and cellular flow sensing.

faculty.name: Dimitrios Vavylonis
faculty.title: Professor of Physics; Iacocca Chair of the College of Arts and Sciences
faculty.summary: Iacocca Chair. Models cytoskeleton dynamics, cellular self-assembly, and biological physics using computational approaches.

faculty.name: Jerome Licini
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Physics
faculty.summary: Focuses on physics education research and experimental physics instrumentation.

programs.program_name: Health Sciences and Technology - Medical Engineering and Medical Physics (PhD)

funding.note: Fully supported (living expenses, tuition, health insurance)

faculty.name: Elfar Adalsteinsson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Eaton-Peabody Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and IMES at MIT. Leads the Magnetic Resonance Imaging Group at MIT's Research Laboratory of Electronics, developing novel MRI acquisition and reconstruction methods with a focus on fetal and pediatric imaging.

faculty.name: Daniel G. Anderson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Chemical Engineering and IMES at MIT. Leads a lab at the Koch Institute for Integrative Cancer Research focused on nanomedicine, gene therapy, genome editing, biomaterials, and tissue engineering for therapeutic applications.

faculty.name: Sangeeta N. Bhatia
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: John J. and Dorothy Wilson Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and IMES at MIT. Directs the Laboratory for Multiscale Regenerative Technologies, developing nanotechnology platforms for cancer detection and treatment, and engineering liver tissue for regenerative medicine applications.

faculty.name: Berkin Bilgic
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Radiology at Massachusetts General Hospital and Harvard Medical School. Leads the MRI Data Acquisition and Image Reconstruction Lab at the Athinoula A. Martinos Center for Biomedical Imaging, developing accelerated MRI acquisition and deep learning-based reconstruction methods.

faculty.name: Emery N. Brown
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Computational Neuroscience and Health Sciences and Technology at MIT, and Warren M. Zapol Professor of Anaesthesia at Harvard Medical School and MGH. Directs the Neuroscience Statistics Research Laboratory, using statistical and signal processing methods to study how anesthetics act in the brain.

faculty.name: Kwanghun Chung
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Chemical Engineering and IMES at MIT. Pioneer of the CLARITY tissue clearing technology that enables whole-brain imaging with molecular resolution. Develops novel technologies for interrogating complex biological systems at multiple scales.

faculty.name: James J. Collins
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Termeer Professor of Biological Engineering and IMES at MIT. Pioneers synthetic biology approaches to engineer living cells for therapeutic applications, including using deep learning to discover new antibiotics and designing engineered probiotics for diagnostics and treatment.

faculty.name: Elazer R. Edelman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Edward J. Poitras Professor in Medical Engineering and Science at MIT, Director of the Center for Clinical Translational Research. His lab develops medical devices and biomaterials-based therapies for cardiovascular disease, focusing on vascular biology, mechanical organ support, and drug-eluting stents.

faculty.name: Marzyeh Ghassemi
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Herman L. F. von Helmholtz Career Development Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and IMES at MIT. Leads the Healthy Machine Learning Lab, developing robust and fair machine learning models for clinical applications, with a focus on ensuring models perform equitably across diverse patient populations.

faculty.name: Martha L. Gray
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Whitaker Professor in Biomedical Engineering, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and IMES at MIT. Focuses on biomedical technology innovation and translation, with research spanning musculoskeletal biomechanics, medical device development, and biomedical engineering education.

faculty.name: Thomas Heldt
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Richard J. Cohen (1976) Professor in Medicine and Biomedical Physics, Professor of Electrical and Biomedical Engineering at MIT EECS, and Associate Director of IMES. Leads the Integrative Neuromonitoring and Critical Care Informatics Group, developing real-time physiological monitoring and inference algorithms for neurocritical care.

faculty.name: Robert D. Howe
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Abbott and James Lawrence Professor of Engineering at Harvard School of Engineering and Applied Sciences. Leads the Harvard Biorobotics Lab, developing robotic systems for surgery and rehabilitation, including tactile sensing, haptic feedback interfaces, and image-guided intervention technologies.

faculty.name: Susie Y. Huang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Radiology at Massachusetts General Hospital and Harvard Medical School. Develops advanced diffusion MRI methods to probe brain tissue microstructure, enabling noninvasive measurement of axon diameter and white matter integrity for neurological disease characterization.

faculty.name: Robert Langer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Institute Professor and Professor of Chemical Engineering and Biological Engineering at MIT. A pioneer in drug delivery and biomaterials, his lab has developed numerous controlled-release technologies, nanoparticle-based therapies, and tissue engineering platforms that have led to many approved medical products.

faculty.name: Lauren Jean O'Donnell
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Radiology at Brigham and Women's Hospital and Harvard Medical School. Develops computational methods for diffusion MRI tractography and brain connectomics, using machine learning to map white matter pathways and study brain connectivity in health and disease.

faculty.name: Ellen Roche
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Mechanical Engineering and IMES at MIT. Leads the Therapeutic Technology and Device Development Lab, creating innovative minimally invasive cardiac devices using soft robotics, biomimetic design, and mechanobiological principles for treating heart failure and other conditions.

faculty.name: Bruce R. Rosen
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Laurence Lamson Robbins Professor of Radiology at Massachusetts General Hospital and Harvard Medical School. A pioneer in functional MRI, he directs the Athinoula A. Martinos Center for Biomedical Imaging and the Quantitative Tumor Imaging Lab, advancing neuroimaging and cancer imaging methods.

faculty.name: Alex K. Shalek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: J.W. Kieckhefer Professor of IMES and Chemistry at MIT, Director of IMES and the Health Innovation Hub. Develops nanobiotechnology and microfluidics platforms for single-cell analysis, applying these to study cellular heterogeneity in infectious disease, cancer, and immunology toward precision medicine.

faculty.name: David E. Sosnovik
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Medicine at Massachusetts General Hospital and Harvard Medical School. Directs the Program in Cardiovascular Magnetic Resonance at the Martinos Center, developing multimodal cardiac imaging approaches combining MRI, molecular imaging, and optics to study myocardial structure and function.

faculty.name: Collin M. Stultz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Nina T. and Robert H. Rubin Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and IMES at MIT, Co-Director of HST. Leads the Computational Cardiovascular Research Group, developing explainable machine learning models to predict adverse cardiovascular events and guide clinical decision-making.

faculty.name: Guillermo J. Tearney
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor of Pathology at Massachusetts General Hospital and Harvard Medical School. Leads the Tearney Lab at the Wellman Center for Photomedicine, developing next-generation optical coherence tomography and microscopy technologies for in vivo imaging of coronary arteries, the gastrointestinal tract, and other tissues.

faculty.name: Mehmet Toner
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Helen Andrus Benedict Professor of Surgery at Massachusetts General Hospital and Harvard Medical School. Directs the BioMEMS Resource Center, developing microfluidic devices for rare cell isolation (including circulating tumor cells), point-of-care diagnostics, and cell preservation technologies.

faculty.name: Benjamin J. Vakoc
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor of Dermatology at Massachusetts General Hospital and Harvard Medical School. faculty.name: M. Zahid Hasan

faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Bogdan A. Bernevig
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Dmitry Abanin
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Duncan Haldane
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Shinsei Ryu
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: David Huse
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pioneering researcher in many-body localization, quantum chaos, and quantum thermalization, studying how disorder and interactions affect equilibration in isolated quantum systems.

faculty.name: Igor Klebanov
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Leading string theorist with over 210 papers and 25,000+ citations, known for foundational work on gauge/gravity duality, higher-spin AdS/CFT correspondence, and the Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev model.

faculty.name: Herman L. Verlinde
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on string theory, quantum gravity, black hole physics, and the gauge/gravity duality, contributing to foundational understanding of holography and quantum aspects of spacetime.

faculty.name: Silviu Pufu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches conformal field theories, supersymmetric gauge theories, entanglement entropy, and the AdS/CFT correspondence, with extensive work on higher-spin theories and adjoint QCD.

faculty.name: Simone Giombi
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on higher-spin gauge theory, holography, and conformal field theories, with notable contributions to higher-spin AdS4/CFT3 duality and the Klebanov-Polyakov conjecture. Serves as Associate Chair and Director of Graduate Studies.

faculty.name: James Olsen
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Christopher Tully

faculty.name: Jo Dunkley

faculty.name: Lyman A. Page Jr.
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Suzanne T. Staggs
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Frans Pretorius
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Paul J. Steinhardt
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Waseem Bakr
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Michael Romalis
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: William Bialek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: John Archibald Wheeler/Battelle Chair in Physics applying theoretical physics and information theory to understand biological systems, from neural coding in the brain to gene regulatory networks and animal behavior.

faculty.name: Cristiano Galbiati
faculty.title: Professor

faculty.name: Sanfeng Wu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor

faculty.name: Biao Lian
